title: fix: expression injection in KernelManager context wrapping
description:
kernel.py line 182: The string escaping for ToExpression context wrapping is insufficient. An expression containing a double-quote can break out of the ToExpression string and execute arbitrary Wolfram code.

**Exploit:** Pass `expr = ]; Quit[];` (a string containing `"]; Quit[];"`)
- After escaping: `\"]; Quit[];\"` 
- Wrapped: `ToExpression["\"]; Quit[];\""]`
- Wolfram parses this as: `ToExpression[""]` then `; Quit[];` then leftover `""]`
- Result: `Quit[]` executes, killing the kernel

**Root cause:** The escaping handles `\` and `"` characters but the result still allows the Wolfram parser to see unbalanced brackets that close the `ToExpression[...]` call.

**Fix:** Use wolframclient native expression construction (WLFunction, WLSymbol) instead of string concatenation, OR encode the expression as base64 and decode inside Wolfram: `ToExpression[FromCharacterCode[ImportString["<base64>", "Base64"]]]`.

**Test:** Add a test that passes an expression containing `"]; Print[injected]"` with a context_id and verifies the entire expression is evaluated as one string (no injection). The mock should receive a single evaluate call, not multiple.

Inherited from sxAct oracle (same bug exists there).
close_reason: Closed

title: [H1] Narrow except Exception in IsolatedRunner to preserve programming errors
description:
isolation.py:74-77 and :96-106 catch all Exception types and reduce them to str(exc), losing exception type and traceback. A TypeError from a bug in the adapter looks identical to a ConnectionRefusedError.

Fix:
(1) Narrow the except clause — catch only operational exceptions (OSError, ConnectionError, TimeoutError, and AdapterError once M1 lands). Let programming errors (TypeError, AttributeError) propagate.
(2) Include exception class name in error string: f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"

Note: enriching error messages with operation index/action is M3's scope, not this ticket.

Acceptance criteria:
- Write a test where adapter.execute() raises TypeError — verify it propagates out of IsolatedRunner.run() instead of being captured as a TestRunResult.error
- Write a test where adapter.execute() raises OSError — verify it IS captured as TestRunResult.error with the class name in the message
- Run pytest tests/test_isolation.py to confirm no regressions
close_reason: Implemented in commits 22410c0 and 10a808d. All tests pass.

title: Expected evaluation: verdict logic for test outcomes
description: IsolatedRunner returns raw tokens but nobody evaluates the Expected dataclass from the bridge. Add verdict logic that takes the last token from a test run and compares it against TestCase.expected fields (expr, normalized, is_zero, properties, comparison_tier, expect_error). Returns pass/fail with diagnostics. This is the blocker for sxAct to consume elegua — without it, sxAct cannot drop its IsolatedContext. Reference: sxAct/packages/sxact/src/sxact/runner/isolation.py _evaluate_expected().
close_reason: evaluate_expected() implemented: checks expr, normalized, is_zero, value, properties, expect_error. Injectable normalizer for domain-specific comparison. TestRunResult now carries bindings for $-substitution. 26 new tests, 244 total passing.

title: Multi-tier verification: Oracle vs IUT cross-comparison
description: Implement MultiTierRunner that executes the same TestFile on an oracle adapter and one or more IUT adapters, then compares results per test via ComparisonPipeline. Returns VerificationResult per test with oracle/IUT tokens and comparison verdict. This is the core spec requirement: 'Eleguá SHALL execute a symbolic task across multiple Tiers to ensure mathematical parity.'
close_reason: MultiTierRunner implemented: runs TestFile on oracle + IUT adapters independently, compares last token per test via ComparisonPipeline, returns VerificationResult with both sides + verdict. Handles errors, skips, empty files. 9 new tests, 216 total passing.

title: [epic] Epic: sxAct→Elegua Migration Integration
description: Umbrella epic for all work needed to make Elegua a fully functional orchestrator that can replace sxAct's runner/adapter/compare infrastructure. Covers adapter lifecycle, real adapters, TOML bridge, comparison layer plugins, context isolation, snapshot/replay, and IPC transport. Cross-project counterpart: sxAct epic 'Elegua Integration' tracks the sxAct-side extraction and refactor work.
close_reason: All children complete. Elegua core orchestrator is fully functional: adapter lifecycle, TOML bridge, execution context, isolation runner, Wolfram oracle adapter, pluggable comparison pipeline, snapshot record/replay. IPC transport deferred (HTTP covers all current needs). 207 tests passing.

title: TOML schema bridge: load sxAct test format into EleguaTask
description: sxAct TOML format is richer than Elegua simple tasks array. sxAct has: [[setup]] actions for environment prep, [[tests]] with [[tests.operations]] containing action/args/store_as, [tests.expected] with expr/normalized/comparison_tier, and metadata like oracle_is_axiom and layer tags. Write a loader (or extend the existing one) that parses the sxAct schema and emits EleguaTask sequences. Reference schema: sxAct/packages/sxact/src/sxact/runner/schemas/test-schema.json
close_reason: Bridge loader implemented: parses sxAct TOML format (meta, setup, tests, operations, expected) into structured data models. TestFile.to_tasks() flattens to EleguaTask sequences. 26 new tests, 123 total passing.

title: Real WolframAdapter: wire OracleClient HTTP to Elegua Adapter
description: Replace the stub WolframAdapter with a real implementation that uses sxAct OracleClient (HTTP client to Flask server at :8765 to WSTP WolframLanguageSession). Translate EleguaTask (action + payload) into Wolfram expressions, send via OracleClient.evaluate_with_xact(), and map the sxAct Result dataclass back to Elegua ValidationToken. Depends on adapter lifecycle hooks for kernel startup/shutdown.
close_reason: WolframOracleAdapter implemented with OracleClient (stdlib urllib), build_expr for 30+ xAct actions, result mapping to ValidationToken, Assert special handling, lifecycle with health/cleanup. 27 new tests, 176 total passing.

title: Adapter lifecycle: add initialize() and teardown() to ABC
description: The sxAct TestAdapter has initialize(), execute(), teardown() lifecycle. Elegua Adapter ABC only has execute(). Add optional initialize() and teardown() methods with default no-op implementations so existing adapters do not break. The WolframAdapter needs initialize() to start the kernel/HTTP connection and teardown() to clean up. Smallest change that unblocks all other integration work.
close_reason: Added initialize()/teardown() with no-op defaults + context manager support. Runner now brackets execution with lifecycle calls. 97 tests passing, lint and types clean.

title: Strip tensor-specific coupling during extraction from sxAct
description: When extracting runner/adapter/compare/oracle code from sxAct, remove all tensor-algebra-specific assumptions. Replace with domain-agnostic interfaces (e.g., generic Expression type, pluggable normalizer registry, adapter ABC with no xAct imports). This is what makes Elegua reusable for Rubi, FeynCalc, etc.
close_reason: N/A — elegua was built from scratch with no sxAct extraction. Source has zero tensor/xAct references. Already domain-agnostic by construction.

title: fix: silent failure in check_clean_state string parsing
description:
kernel.py lines 149-157: The check_clean_state method parses a string response from Wolfram with format "G:0,sym1,sym2". If the response is malformed (kernel error, unexpected output), the broad `except (FuturesTimeout, Exception)` clause silently returns `(False, ["check_clean_state evaluation failed"])` with no indication of what went wrong.

Fix: Log the actual result and exception before returning the fallback. Narrow the exception handler to catch only expected exceptions.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: snapshot conftest function-scoped fixture loses data across tests
description:
tests/integration/conftest.py lines 31-42: The snapshot_store fixture is function-scoped but computes its file path from the module name. Each test gets a NEW empty SnapshotStore instance. In RECORD_MODE, recordings from one test are lost when the next test creates a fresh instance (SnapshotStore(path) does not load existing data).

Fix: Either make the fixture module-scoped (shared across tests in the module), or load existing snapshots before recording: use SnapshotStore.read(path) as the starting point in record mode too.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: replace assertions with explicit checks in kernel.py
description:
kernel.py lines 115, 145, 194: Uses `assert self._session is not None` inside _do_eval closures submitted to the ThreadPoolExecutor. Assertions are stripped when Python runs with -O flag, which would cause AttributeError on None instead of a clear error.

Fix: Replace with `if self._session is None: raise RuntimeError("Kernel session not started")` for all three occurrences.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: global KernelManager in server.py has no shutdown hook
description:
server.py lines 22-23: KernelManager is created at module import time as a global. The serve() function never calls km.stop() on exit, and there are no signal handlers for SIGTERM/SIGINT. The Wolfram kernel process is leaked when the server shuts down.

Fix:
1. Keep `km` as a module-level variable (Flask route handlers reference it), but initialize it lazily in serve() or at first request
2. Register `atexit.register(km.stop)` in serve() for clean shutdown
3. Register signal handlers for SIGTERM and SIGINT that call km.stop() then sys.exit()
4. Alternatively, use Flask before_first_request / teardown_appcontext hooks

Depends on elegua-5i3 (stop() must properly shut down the executor first).
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: ThreadPoolExecutor never shut down in KernelManager
description:
kernel.py line 55: The ThreadPoolExecutor created in __init__ is never shut down. KernelManager.stop() sets _session=None but does not call _executor.shutdown(). This leaks threads when the KernelManager is garbage collected or the server shuts down.

Fix:
1. Add `self._executor.shutdown(wait=False)` in `stop()` before setting `_session = None`
2. Add context manager support (`__enter__`/`__exit__`) to KernelManager that calls `stop()` on exit
3. Do NOT use `__del__` — it is unreliable in Python and not guaranteed to run

Test: Add a test that creates a KernelManager, calls stop(), and verifies the executor is shut down (check `_executor._shutdown` attribute).
close_reason: Closed

comparison.py:86-97: if no layers are registered, compare() silently returns ComparisonResult(status=MATH_MISMATCH, layer=0, layer_name=''). This is a silent false-negative — no layers ran, yet the caller receives a 'mismatch' verdict.

Preferred fix: raise a descriptive error (e.g., PipelineMisconfiguredError or ValueError) when compare() is called with an empty _layers list. Fail fast on misconfigured pipelines rather than producing misleading results.

The docstring says teardown must not raise, but there is no enforcement. Fix: wrap self.teardown() in try/except that logs/warns but does not mask the original exception. No module in the codebase uses exception chaining. Every place an exception is caught and a new one raised should use 'raise NewError(...) from original' to preserve the causal chain.

- runner.py load_toml_tasks(): raise SchemaError(...) from exc
- isolation.py except clauses: raise AdapterError(...) from exc
- snapshot.py read(): raise ... from exc
- blobstore.py get(): raise ... from exc

Acceptance criteria:

- Tests verify __cause__ is set on chained exceptions
- Run full pytest suite

title: [M1] Introduce domain exception hierarchy replacing generic ValueError/RuntimeError
description:
The codebase inconsistently uses ValueError (bridge.py, runner.py) vs domain exceptions (PropertyValidationError, InvalidTransition).

Implementation:
1. Create src/elegua/errors.py with the hierarchy:
   - EleguaError(Exception) — base for all domain errors
   - SchemaError(EleguaError, ValueError) — TOML/validation errors (replaces bare ValueError in bridge.py, runner.py)
   - AdapterError(EleguaError) — adapter execution failures (used by isolation.py, wolfram.py)
   - OracleError(AdapterError) — oracle-specific failures (oracle.py, wolfram.py)
2. Reparent existing exceptions:
   - PropertyValidationError(SchemaError) instead of (Exception)
   - InvalidTransition(EleguaError) instead of (Exception)
3. Replace bare ValueError in bridge.py:105,128,131,134,156,160,162 and runner.py:22,35 with SchemaError
4. Inherit from ValueError where needed for backward compatibility

Acceptance criteria:
- isinstance(SchemaError(...), ValueError) is True (backward compat)
- isinstance(PropertyValidationError(...), EleguaError) is True
- All existing tests still pass after reparenting
- New tests in test_errors.py verifying the hierarchy
- Run full pytest suite
close_reason: Implemented in commits 22410c0 and 10a808d. All tests pass.

title: IPC transport layer: subprocess + ZMQ/TCP adapter communication
description: Implement the multi-protocol IPC strategy from the design doc. Subprocess mode for quick one-off kernels (stdin/stdout). ZMQ/TCP mode for persistent long-lived symbolic kernels (Wolfram Engine, Julia). The Adapter ABC should be transport-agnostic — adapters declare which transport they need, and the runner provisions it. This replaces sxAct's direct HTTP-to-Flask-to-WSTP chain with a pluggable transport.
notes: Deferred 2026-03-19: HTTP oracle transport covers all current needs (Flask+WSTP persistent kernel, context isolation, health/cleanup). No concrete consumer requires subprocess or ZMQ today. Revisit when: (1) a non-HTTP backend appears (e.g., Julia subprocess adapter), (2) high-throughput property testing needs ZMQ, or (3) a lighter-than-HTTP transport is needed for simple backends.

Enables 'Record & Replay' mode: run once against live Wolfram oracle, persist results as blobs, then replay in CI without a Wolfram license. Reference: sxAct/packages/sxact/src/sxact/snapshot/ for the existing snapshot runner, store, and writer. The elegua blob store (SHA-256 content-addressable) already provides the storage layer — this ticket adds the capture/replay orchestration on top.

title: Context isolation: UUID-scoped Wolfram context per test

Post-test cleanup resets the Global context and xAct registries. Add isolation support to the adapter lifecycle — initialize() sets up a scoped context, teardown() cleans it up. Reference: sxAct/packages/sxact/src/sxact/runner/isolation.py

title: Domain-specific comparison layers: symbolic and numeric for sxAct
description: Register sxAct comparison logic as Elegua Layers 3-4. Layer 3 (Canonical): send Simplify[lhs - rhs] === 0 to the Wolfram oracle for symbolic equivalence. Layer 4 (Invariant): numeric sampling with random index substitution for expressions with free indices. The comparison pipeline needs a registration mechanism for domain-specific layers. Reference: sxAct/packages/sxact/src/sxact/compare/comparator.py and sampling.py
close_reason: ComparisonPipeline with pluggable layer registration. L1/L2 built-in by default, L3/L4 registered via register(). Layers run in order, stop at first match. Closures enable domain-specific context injection. 13 new tests, 190 total passing.

title: Execution context: variable store for store_as chaining
description: sxAct tests chain operations via store_as: the result of one operation is stored by name and referenced in later operations. The runner needs a per-test-suite context dict that adapters can read from and write to. This enables multi-step test sequences like DefManifold, DefMetric, DefTensor, ToCanonical where each step depends on prior definitions.

This is the standard RUBI verification strategy that works today with no code changes via store_as + dollar-ref mechanism. Include in adapter guidelines or a new verification-patterns doc.

title: fix: adapter execute() works without initialize() (silent context leak)
description:
adapter.py lines 64, 109: _context_id is initialized to None in __init__. If execute() is called without initialize() (user forgets context manager), _context_id=None is passed to the kernel, which silently evaluates in the Global context instead of an isolated namespace. This violates the isolation contract.

Fix: Check _context_id is not None in execute() and raise RuntimeError if initialize() was not called.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: oracle_adapter fixture does not guarantee snapshot persistence
description:
tests/integration/conftest.py lines 45-55: The oracle_adapter fixture returns a RecordingAdapter but does not manage its lifecycle. Tests must use `with oracle_adapter:` to trigger teardown() which writes snapshots. If a test uses the adapter without a context manager, teardown() is never called and recordings are lost silently.

Fix: Convert to a yield fixture that calls adapter.__enter__() before yield and adapter.__exit__() after, or use request.addfinalizer() to guarantee cleanup.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: no validation of ELEGUA_WOLFRAM_INIT path at startup
description:
kernel.py line 58: The init script path from ELEGUA_WOLFRAM_INIT is stored but never validated. If the file does not exist, the error only surfaces at runtime when _ensure_init() calls Get[] on the Wolfram kernel, producing an opaque Wolfram error.

Fix: Validate the file exists and is readable in __init__. Raise a clear error at startup rather than a confusing kernel error at evaluation time.
close_reason: Closed

title: [L6] Fix maybe_resolve semantics: don't raise on missing blob ref
description:
blobstore.py:56-59 maybe_resolve raises FileNotFoundError on missing blob despite the maybe_ prefix implying optional/safe semantics.

Preferred fix: catch FileNotFoundError inside maybe_resolve and return the original payload unchanged, with a warnings.warn() for traceability. The 'maybe_' prefix should mean 'resolve if possible, pass through otherwise.'

Acceptance criteria:
- maybe_resolve with a missing blob ref returns the original {'blob': 'sha...'} dict
- A warning is emitted when the blob is not found
- maybe_resolve with an existing blob still resolves correctly
- Run pytest tests/test_blobstore.py

title: [L3] Validate store_as values are valid \w+ identifiers
description:
context.py and bridge.py:109 accept any string as store_as, but resolve_refs uses the regex \$(\w+) to match references. A store_as value with spaces or special characters will never match on resolution, causing silent failures.

Fix: add validation at parse time in bridge.py _parse_operation that store_as matches the pattern [a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]* (a valid Python identifier / \w+ match).

Acceptance criteria:
- _parse_operation raises SchemaError (or ValueError) for store_as='has spaces'
- _parse_operation raises for store_as='123starts_with_digit'
- Valid identifiers like 'result_1' and '_private' still accepted
- Run pytest tests/test_bridge.py
close_reason: Implemented with tests. L1-L6, L9, L10 all done.
